cases.title: Any type — every value passes
BigInt primitive
cases.description: Infinity and -Infinity rejected (typeof gate)

cases.title: Boolean primitive (strict typeof)
Date instance (rejects Invalid Date)
cases.description: Invalid Date instances (getTime() === NaN) rejected

cases.title: Enum with mixed numeric and string members
cases.description: enum Color {Red, Green="green", Blue=2} — numeric reverse-mapping + string values

cases.title: Numeric literal type (strict equality)
String literal type (case-sensitive)
Boolean literal type (only true)
BigInt literal type (only 1n)
Symbol literal type (matched by description)
cases.description: symbol identity via description match (mion semantics)

cases.title: Never — no value passes
Null primitive (distinct from undefined)
cases.description: null and undefined are distinct

cases.title: Number primitive (rejects NaN and Infinity)
cases.description: Infinity and -Infinity rejected (Number.isFinite)

cases.title: Object type — any non-null non-primitive value
cases.description: null rejected despite JS typeof null === "object"

cases.title: RegExp instance
String primitive
Symbol primitive
Undefined primitive (distinct from null)
cases.description: undefined and null are distinct

cases.title: Void — accepts undefined, rejects null
cases.description: void accepts undefined (and bare function return); rejects null

cases.title: Numeric literal with noLiterals (degrades to number)
cases.description: degrades to number — Number.isFinite check

cases.title: String literal with noLiterals (degrades to string)
cases.description: degrades to string — typeof check

cases.title: RegExp literal with noLiterals (degrades to RegExp)
cases.description: degrades to RegExp — instanceof check

cases.title: Boolean literal with noLiterals (degrades to boolean)
cases.description: degrades to boolean — typeof check

cases.title: BigInt literal with noLiterals (degrades to bigint)
cases.description: degrades to bigint — typeof check

cases.title: Symbol literal with noLiterals (degrades to symbol)
cases.description: degrades to bare symbol — unsupported at root

cases.title: Unknown type — every value passes
Array of strings
Array of numbers (rejects Infinity / NaN per element)
cases.description: Infinity / -Infinity / NaN rejected per atomic-number port

cases.title: Array of booleans
Array of bigints
Array of Dates (rejects Invalid Date per element)
cases.description: from mion serialization-suite ARRAYS.array_date

cases.title: Array of RegExps
Array of undefined values
cases.description: from mion serialization-suite ARRAYS.undefined_in_array

cases.title: Array of nulls
Generic Array<T> form (same emit as T[])
cases.description: TypeScript sugar — resolves identically to string[]; carried as a regression check on canonical-id collapse

cases.title: Two-dimensional string array (multi-level dependency call)
cases.description: first multi-level test — exercises the Go-side dependency-call layer (outer array invokes pre-compiled inner via utl.getRT(...).fn(v[i0]))

cases.title: Three-dimensional string array (depth stress)
cases.description: depth stress for the dependency-call layer

cases.title: Array with noIsArrayCheck (Array.isArray guard stripped)
cases.description: noIsArrayCheck strips the Array.isArray guard; hashes distinctly from plain string_array — same samples, different validator

cases.title: Array of object literals
cases.description: mion array.spec.ts 'test array strict modes' — array of objects. Extra keys on object elements still pass validate (unknown-key handling is a different adapter).

cases.title: Array of unions (OR-chain per element)
cases.description: array of union — each element validates against the union OR-chain.

cases.title: Array of tuples
cases.description: array of tuples — exercises tuple under array dependency call.

cases.title: Self-referential array (CircularArray = CircularArray[])
cases.description: mion array.spec.ts 'Array circular ref'. Self-referential array — handled via the always-non-inlined KindArray policy plus the isSelf branch in EmitDependencyCall (emits the inner-function-name directly, no .fn).

cases.title: Recursive object whose cycle closes via an array property
cases.description: type ObjectType = {a: string; deep?: {b: string; c: number}; d?: ObjectType[]} — same dependency-call mechanism as the basic circular interface; the array property d?: ObjectType[] closes the cycle via Array → Object.

cases.title: Array of symbols (non-serializable — factory throws)
cases.description: mion ARRAYS.non_serializable_in_array — `Arrays can not have non serializable types` (nodes/member/array.ts:148): mion throws at RT-compile. The port propagates CodeNS from the symbol element to the root, rendering an alwaysThrow factory (T3), so createValidate<symbol[]>() / createGetValidationErrors<symbol[]>() throw on first call — consistent with the unified rule (non-property positions throw). As a *property* child a non-serializable array drops the property instead.

cases.title: Readonly array (ReadonlyArray<T> / readonly T[])
cases.description: `readonly T[]` and `ReadonlyArray<T>` are the same type at runtime — the readonly bit is a TS-only modifier erased at emit. Regression check that both forms produce the same validator as the bare `T[]` shape.

cases.title: Simple interface with string and number props
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts "validate object" (simplified to the atomic-prop subset that the current Go port can validate end-to-end)

cases.title: Object pinned with `as const` (readonly literal props)
cases.description: Object literal pinned with `as const` — every property becomes a readonly literal type. Verifies that the type-id resolution and validator emit handle the readonly-literal-props shape end-to-end and that the static / reflect forms agree.

cases.title: Object inferred via ReturnType<typeof factory>
cases.description: Static-form usage of the recommended `ReturnType<typeof fn>` idiom when you have a factory function whose return type you want to validate. The reflect form `createValidate(makeUser())` would invoke the function at runtime purely for type inference — anti-pattern that the resolver now flags as a build-time warning. The reflect-form thunk is intentionally omitted; the diagnostic test in vite-plugin-runtypes covers the warning.

cases.title: Object inferred via property access on a parent shape
cases.description: Reflect form with a property-access argument (`createValidate(outer.user)`). T comes from the property's declared type on the parent shape — property accesses don't go through const-binding CFA, so the natural pattern produces the same hash as the static form.

cases.title: Object inferred via array element access
cases.description: Reflect form with an array-element-access argument (`createValidate(items[0])`). T comes from the array's declared element type — indexed accesses don't go through const-binding CFA, so the natural pattern produces the same hash as the static form.

cases.title: Interface with one optional property
cases.description: optional property — `(v.b === undefined || Number.isFinite(v.b))` per PropertyRunType.emitIsType

cases.title: Interface with a Date property
cases.description: tests that Date child validates via instanceof inside the AND chain — mion interface.spec.ts ObjectType subset

cases.title: Interface with a method (function prop skipped from check)
cases.description: mion: objectSkipProps — function-typed properties are skipped from validate (mion's `getRTChild → undefined` for function children). validate({name:'x'}) PASSES even without `cb`.

cases.title: Interface with a nested object property
cases.description: nested object — outer + inner AND-chains; mion ObjectType "deep" subset

cases.title: Interface with a string-array property
cases.description: an array-typed property — exercises the dependency-call layer through an object

cases.title: Self-referential interface (linked-list shape)
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts 'validate circular object'. Exercises self-recursive dependency call (mion isSelf branch — `<innerFnName>(v.child)` without `.fn`).

cases.title: Self-referential interface via an array-of-self property
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts 'validate circular interface on array' — circular type traversed via an array property.

cases.title: Self-referential interface buried in a nested object
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts 'validate circular interface on nested object' — circular reference deep inside a property.

cases.title: Index signature with string values
cases.description: mion indexProperty.spec.ts 'validate index run type' — for-in loop over own keys, value must satisfy the value type.

cases.title: Index signature combined with named properties
cases.description: mion indexProperty.spec.ts 'validate index run type + extra properties' — named props (a, b) AND the index signature both validate; extras (any key not a/b) must satisfy the union value type.

cases.title: Nested index signatures (number leaf values)
cases.description: mion indexProperty.spec.ts nested rtNested — index sig pointing at another index sig.

cases.title: Nested index signatures with Date leaf values
cases.description: mion indexProperty.spec.ts rtNested2 — Date as the leaf value type.

cases.title: Index signature on a nested (non-root) object property
cases.description: mion indexProperty.spec.ts 'IndexType non root' — index signature attached to a nested (non-root) object property.

cases.title: Function type at top level (any function passes)
cases.description: mion FunctionRunType.emitIsType — `typeof v === 'function'`. Param-arity check is deferred (mion-level).

cases.title: Callable interface (function plus data properties)
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts "validate callable interface" — the emit detects a CallSignature child and switches the typeof guard from `object` to `function`, then AND-chains the remaining properties on top (JS functions can carry properties).

cases.title: Interface with every property optional (plain-object guard)
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts "validate empty object for ObjectAllOptional type". The `allOptionalCode` guard `(!Array.isArray(v) && Object.prototype.toString.call(v) === '[object Object]')` is added when every contributing child is optional, so arrays / Date / Map / Set are explicitly rejected (without the guard they'd slip through the bare `typeof === 'object'` check).

cases.title: Class with two atomic props (instance or plain match)
cases.description: mion class.spec.ts 'validate class'. ClassRunType inherits InterfaceRunType.emitIsType in mion, so the KindClass+SubKindNone arm in istype.go falls through to emitObjectValidate. The serializer filters synthetic `prototype` members from class projections so the AND chain only includes user-declared properties + methods (methods drop out via the function-skip rule).

cases.title: RpcError-shaped class with branded discriminator
cases.description: mion classRpcError.spec.ts — verifies the standard class projection handles RpcError-shaped classes (the actual @mionjs/core RpcError isn't a built-in node kind; it's a regular class with a literal-true brand + generic type discriminator). We define a local equivalent here to exercise the same shape end-to-end without pulling in the @mionjs/core dependency for a single test.

cases.title: Function parameters extracted via Parameters<F>
cases.description: mion callSignature.spec.ts 'should validate correct parameters' — mion exposes this via `rt.getCallSignature().createRTParamsFunction(RTFunctions.validate)`; our pipeline uses TypeScript's built-in `Parameters<F>` to extract the param tuple as a first-class type and reuses the standard tuple emit. Same observable behavior: the validator accepts `[number, boolean]`, rejects wrong-type args, accepts missing trailing args (treats them as undefined per mion's `v.length <= N` policy), rejects excess args.

cases.title: Parameters<F> tuple with a trailing optional argument
cases.description: mion function.spec.ts 'validate function parameters' — params tuple with a trailing optional. `Parameters<F>` resolves to `[number, boolean, string?]`; the optional slot accepts undefined OR a string.

cases.title: Parameters<F> tuple with a trailing rest segment
cases.description: mion function.spec.ts 'validate function with rest parameters' — params tuple ending in a rest segment. `Parameters<F>` resolves to `[number, boolean, ...Date[]]`; all trailing slots must satisfy Date.

cases.title: Record<UnionKey, V> — resolves to a fixed-property shape
cases.description: `Record<K, V>` with a literal-union key resolves to a fixed-property object literal (`{a: V; b: V}`) at the type-checker level — tsgo distributes the union over the property names. Same emit path as a hand-written object literal; each key is a required property of type V.

cases.title: Index signature with a union value type
cases.description: index signature with union value type — union emit landed; for-in loop applies the union check to every own key.

cases.title: Object with a discriminated-union string property
cases.description: discriminated union as a property type — union emit handles the literal-string union as an OR-chain of `===` checks.

cases.title: Interface that extends a parent interface
cases.description: TS `interface Child extends Base {…}` — inherited props are merged into the child's RunType.Children by tsgo's GetPropertiesOfType. The validator's emit walks the merged set; runtime behaviour matches a hand-flattened object literal.

cases.title: Class that extends a parent class
cases.description: TS `class Sub extends Base {…}` — same merging as interface inheritance, but on the KindClass branch. Inherited data members appear in the child class's Children alongside its own.

cases.title: Index signature with a number key
cases.description: `{[k: number]: T}` — TS lets you declare number-keyed index signatures. JS object keys are always strings at runtime, so the resolver normalises this to the same shape as `{[k: string]: T}` and the validator behaves identically.

cases.title: Two-element tuple (string plus number)
Six-element heterogeneous tuple (mion fixture)
cases.description: mion tuple.spec.ts "validate tuple"

cases.title: Tuple with trailing optional elements
cases.description: mion tuple.spec.ts "validate tuple with optional parameters"

cases.title: Tuple as array element (tuple inside array dependency call)
cases.description: array of tuples — exercises tuple inside array dependency call

cases.title: Tuple with a trailing rest segment
cases.description: mion tuple.spec.ts 'validate tuple with rest parameter'. Rest TupleMembers (Flags=['rest']) emit a for-loop starting at the member's Position and iterating to v.length, validating every element against the wrapped type. The tuple's length-bound check is skipped (rest absorbs extras).

cases.title: Self-referential tuple via trailing optional self-ref
cases.description: mion tuple.spec.ts circular tuple. Same mechanism as circular array — Tuple is always non-inlined, the self-recursive dependency call closes the cycle via the isSelf branch.

cases.title: Tuple with multiple trailing optional slots
cases.description: Multiple trailing optionals — TS grammar requires optionals to come after required elements (`[A, B?, C]` is a TS error), so the canonical 'optional middle' form is a chain of trailing optionals. Each TupleMember.Optional flag fires its own `(v[i] === undefined || childCheck)` wrap independently.

cases.title: Tuple with named element labels (labels erased at runtime)
cases.description: Named tuple labels — `[name: string, age: number]` is the same shape as `[string, number]` at runtime (labels are TS-only metadata, erased at emit). Carried as a regression check that label syntax doesn't affect the validator shape.

cases.title: Tuple with a function slot (must be undefined)
cases.description: mion serialization-suite TUPLES.tuple_with_non_serializable. Function-typed tuple members emit `v[i] === undefined` per mion's non-serializable handling. The function slot must be absent or explicitly undefined; any other value (a real function, a string, …) fails.

cases.title: Empty tuple `[]` (only the empty array passes)
cases.description: Zero-length tuple — the validator accepts only `[]` (Array.isArray + length === 0). Edge case for the tuple emit; mirrors mion's `children.length === 0` branch.

cases.title: Single-element tuple `[T]`
cases.description: One-slot tuple — corner case for the length-bound check (length must be exactly 1 modulo optional / rest). Exercises the same emit shape as multi-element tuples but with a single member.

cases.title: Readonly tuple (readonly [T, U])
cases.description: `readonly [T, U]` — readonly modifier on a tuple type. As with arrays, the readonly bit is TS-only and erased at runtime; the validator is identical to the bare `[T, U]` shape.

cases.title: Union of common atomic types (with Date and bigint)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "validate union" — Atomic Union suite

cases.title: Union of string literals (case-sensitive)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "validate union discriminator string"

cases.title: Large union (8 heterogeneous arms) — value-first infer fallback
cases.description: Past the 4 positional union() overloads, the value-first builder routes through the recursive UnionOf<T> infer fallback. 8 arms (literals + primitives + a {a}/{a;b} subset+superset pair) verify the fallback BOTH generates a correct validator AND converges on the type-first union id — preserving the subset/superset arms (no subtype collapse) at depth 8.

cases.title: Two-arm union of string and number
Union of array types (whole-array dispatch)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "Union Arr"

cases.title: Array whose element type is a union
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "Arr with union of types"

cases.title: Union of disjoint object shapes
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'Union Obj'. Object-typed union members go through the dependency-call layer with the shared `typeof === 'object' && !== null` guard lifted out of the OR-chain.

cases.title: Discriminated union (shared kind literal, different payloads)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "Union with discriminator property" — the OR-chain is semantically correct; the discriminator-aware optimization (early-return on the discriminator literal) is a separate emit-shape concern handled later.

cases.title: Self-referential union via object and array arms
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "Union circular". Handled via always-non-inlined Union + Object + Array (no IsCircular detection needed; the dependency-call layer terminates via the lazy-init two-phase cache registration).

cases.title: Union of object arms each carrying a method
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts "Union with objects containing methods" — methods are skipped from each branch via the property-emit function-skip rule (the AND chain inside each object reduces to the data-only props).

cases.title: Intersection of object shapes (resolved to one merged shape)
cases.description: mion intersection.spec.ts — tsgo / deepkit resolves intersections to ObjectLiteral at the type-checker level, so the cache never carries a KindIntersection that needs validation. Runtime behavior matches `{a: string; b: number}` byte-for-byte.

cases.title: Union where one arm carries an index signature
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'validate an union with index property' — arm carries a named prop AND an index signature; index-typed extras are accepted alongside the named prop.

cases.title: Discriminated union sharing one prop with arm-dependent type
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'validate union same prop with different types' — same prop name (`prop`) carries an arm-dependent value type, gated by the literal-string discriminator.

cases.title: Union mixing array types and object shapes
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'Union Mixed' — arrays and objects in the same union; the OR-chain dispatches on shape (Array.isArray vs object typeof).

cases.title: Union of shapes sharing a prop with different value types
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'validate union with merged properties' — single shared prop with different value types; `a` accepts boolean OR number.

cases.title: Union mixing arrays, plain objects, and index-signature shapes
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'Union mixed with index property' — arrays + objects (some with index signatures) in the same union.

cases.title: Union with an `any` arm (collapses to any)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'support union with any type' — tsgo collapses `T | any` to `any`, so any value passes (the validator is effectively a no-op true).

cases.title: Union with an `unknown` arm (collapses to unknown)
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'support union with unknown type' — tsgo collapses `T | unknown` to `unknown`, so any value passes.

cases.title: Union with the smaller arm declared before its superset
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'sortUnreachableTypes' — `{a}` defined before `{a; b}`. Both arms must be reachable: matching SmallObj must not swallow LargeObj-shaped inputs (semantically the same since either arm matching returns true, but pins the regression).

cases.title: Union with a three-level subset chain
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'multiple levels of subset relationships' — three arms, each a strict superset of the previous.

cases.title: Union mixing a subset pair with a disjoint arm
cases.description: mion union.spec.ts 'mixed related and unrelated types' — Base and Extended are subset-related, Unrelated is disjoint.

cases.title: Template literal URL with a number placeholder
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'URL pattern api/user/${number}'. Compiled to `^api\/user\/-?(?:\d+\.?\d*|\.\d+)$` at RT-build time; validate emits `typeof v === 'string' && regex.test(v)`.

cases.title: Template literal URL with multiple placeholders
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'multi-segment URL'. Multiple placeholders + literal segments.

cases.title: Template literal starting with a string placeholder
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'leading ${string} placeholder' — empty-string prefix accepted (string span uses `[\s\S]*`, not `+`).

cases.title: Template literal with regex metacharacters in literal segments
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'regex special chars in literal' — parens (and other regex metacharacters) in the literal segments must be escaped in the compiled regex.

cases.title: Object with a template-literal-typed string property
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'nested in object' — template literal as a property value; the parent object's AND chain composes the typeof+regex check against `v.url`.

cases.title: Index signature whose key is a template literal pattern
cases.description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'as index signature key' — index signature whose key type is a template literal pattern. The IndexSignature emit now compiles the key pattern to a regex (same path as standalone template literals) and adds a per-key `regex.test(k)` check to the for-in loop, mirroring mion's getKeyPatternVar.

cases.title: Template literal with a union-of-literals placeholder
cases.description: Template literal with a union placeholder. tsgo distributes the union internally, so the type-checker hands the projector either a union span or a pre-distributed set of template literals; either way the compiled regex must constrain the placeholder to {a, b} — anything outside the union must be rejected.

cases.title: Map with string keys and number values
cases.description: mion native/map — `v instanceof Map` plus iteration over `v.entries()` checking each key and value against K / V.

cases.title: Set of strings
cases.description: mion native/set — `v instanceof Set` plus iteration over `v.values()`.

cases.title: Promise — thenable check, wrapped type not validated
cases.description:
Promise validation is a thenable check — `typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof v.then === 'function'`. The wrapped T cannot be validated synchronously (the promise hasn't resolved); callers use `Awaited

` for the resolved-value check (see `awaited_promise` below). prepareForJson/restoreFromJson throw at RT compile (mion's nodes/native/promise.ts).

cases.title: Awaited<Promise<T>> — resolves to the wrapped type
cases.description:
TypeScript's built-in `Awaited

` utility unwraps the promise to its resolved type; tsgo resolves it at compile time, so this case lands as plain `string` in our cache and reuses the atomic string emit. The test verifies the utility threads through correctly.

cases.title: Self-referential object with optional self-ref and Date prop
cases.description: mion circularRefs.spec.ts 'Circular object' — full mion fixture (number + string + self-ref + Date). Exercises the same self-recursive dependency call as OBJECT.circular_interface but pins the exact mion shape.

cases.title: Self-referential array whose union element includes the array itself
cases.description: mion circularRefs.spec.ts 'Circular array + union' — self-recursive array whose element type is a union including the array itself. Closes the cycle via Array → Union → Array.

cases.title: Self-referential object whose cycle closes via a tuple property
cases.description: mion circularRefs.spec.ts 'Circular object with tuple' — cycle closed via a tuple-typed property. Same mechanism as TUPLE.tuple_circular but the recursion goes through an object → tuple boundary.

cases.title: Self-referential object whose cycle closes via an index signature
cases.description: mion circularRefs.spec.ts 'Circular Object with index property' — cycle closed via an index-signature value type. Exercises the index-signature for-in loop calling back into the same validator.

cases.title: Self-referential object with the cycle buried four levels deep
cases.description: mion circularRefs.spec.ts 'Circular Object with deep nested properties' — cycle closed via four levels of nested object properties. Stresses the dependency-call layer when the self-ref is buried deep in an anonymous-shape chain.

cases.title: Non-circular root holding a circular child interface
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts 'Interface with nested circular type where root is not the circular ref' — RootNotCircular is a flat shape (literal discriminator + one prop) whose ciChild property is a self-referential ICircularDeep. Pins the case where the dependency-call layer kicks in BELOW the root rather than at the root itself.

cases.title: Multiple circular types cross-referenced from a non-circular root
cases.description: mion interface.spec.ts 'Interface with nested circular + multiple circular' — RootCircular carries an optional self-ref AND two distinct circular siblings (ICircularDeep, ICircularDate), and ICircularDate also references ICircularDeep. Stresses the resolver / dependency-call layer when more than one recursive type is in flight at once and the cycles cross.

cases.title: Partial<T> — all props become optional
cases.description: mion utility/partial.spec.ts — all properties become optional. Resolves to {name?: string; age?: number; createdAt?: Date}; reuses the object emit with allOptionalCode array-rejection guard.

cases.title: Required<T> — all optional props become required
cases.description: mion utility/required.spec.ts — all properties become required. Resolves to a plain object literal; reuses the object emit.

cases.title: Pick<T, K> — keeps only the named properties
cases.description: mion utility/pick.spec.ts — selects a subset of properties. Resolves to {name: string; createdAt: Date}.

cases.title: Omit<T, K> — drops the named properties
cases.description: mion utility/omit.spec.ts — removes selected properties. Resolves to {name: string; createdAt: Date}.

cases.title: Exclude<U, X> on a string-literal union
cases.description: mion utility/exclude.spec.ts (atomic case) — excludes union members. Resolves to "name" | "createdAt".

cases.title: Extract<U, X> on a string-literal union
cases.description: mion utility/extract.spec.ts (atomic case) — extracts matching union members. Resolves to "name" | "createdAt".

cases.title: Exclude<U, X> on a discriminated object union
cases.description: mion utility/exclude.spec.ts (object union) — excludes object members from a discriminated union.

cases.title: NonNullable<T> — strips null and undefined from a union
cases.description: mion utility/nonNullable.spec.ts — removes null + undefined from a union.

cases.title: ReturnType<F> — extracts the return type of a function
cases.description: mion utility/params-return.spec.ts — extracts a function's return type. Resolves to Date.

cases.title: Readonly<T> — readonly bit erased at runtime
cases.description: Readonly<T> marks properties readonly at the TS layer; the readonly bit is erased at runtime so the validator behaves identically to the source object. Regression check.

cases.title: Partial<T> intersected with Required<Pick<T, K>> (re-requires one prop)
cases.description: Intersection that flips a property's optionality — `Partial<Person>` makes all props optional, then `& Required<Pick<Person, "name">>` re-requires only `name`. tsgo resolves the intersection to {name: string; age?: number; createdAt?: Date}; reuses the object emit.

cases.title: Omit<T, K> preserves optionality of remaining props
cases.description: Omit preserves the optionality of remaining properties — resolves to {b?: number; c: boolean}.

cases.title: keyof T — resolves to a union of string-literal keys
cases.description: `keyof Person` where Person has `name: string; age: number; createdAt: Date` resolves to the union `"name" | "age" | "createdAt"`. The validator is the union of three string literals.

cases.title: typeof variable — type query on a runtime value
cases.description: `typeof config` where `config` is a bound value resolves to the value's static type. Without `as const` the type is widened (`url: string`, `port: number`); with `as const` it pins to literals. This case verifies the widened path.

cases.title: Indexed access type — Person["name"] resolves to string
cases.description: `T[K]` reads the value type of a property. `Person["name"]` resolves to `string` at the type-checker layer; the validator is identical to the atomic `string` shape. Pins the resolution path through the cache.

cases.title: Conditional type — T extends string ? boolean : number
cases.description: `T extends U ? X : Y` resolves at the type-checker layer to either X or Y depending on T. `IsString<"hello">` resolves to `boolean` here. Validation pins that the conditional threads through to the resolved shape.

cases.title: Custom mapped type — {[K in keyof T]: T[K] | null}
cases.description: A user-authored mapped type that augments every prop with `| null`. Tests that resolver + emit thread custom mapped types correctly; Partial / Required / Pick etc. exercise the same machinery via the built-in utility paths.

cases.title: Mapped type whose value is a conditional — per-prop shape diverges
cases.description: `{[K in keyof T]: FieldFor<T[K]>}` where `FieldFor<X>` is a conditional that produces a different object shape for each input type. The resolver evaluates the conditional per prop at the type-checker layer, so each prop ends up with its own concrete (and different) validator. Stress-tests the "two-different-validations-from-one-mapping" pattern.

cases.title: Distributive conditional — `Wrap<string | number>` → `{w:string} | {w:number}`
cases.description: When a conditional type is applied to a generic union, TS distributes the conditional over each member, producing a union of the per-arm results. `T extends any ? {w: T} : never` applied to `string | number` resolves to `{w: string} | {w: number}`. Validator dispatches through the union emit.

cases.title: DeepPartial<T> — recursive mapped type with nested optionality
cases.description: `type DeepPartial<T> = {[K in keyof T]?: T[K] extends object ? DeepPartial<T[K]> : T[K]}`. Recursively makes every nested object-typed property optional. The resolver evaluates the recursion at the type-checker layer; the validator sees the fully flattened all-optional-deep shape.

cases.title: Key prefix via template literal — `prefix_${K}` rename
cases.description: TS 4.1+ key remapping: `{[K in keyof T as `prefix_${K & string}`]: T[K]}`. Resolves to a fully concrete object literal with renamed keys; each value type is carried over unchanged. Common pattern for DB column-name prefixing (`user_id`, `user_name`).

cases.title: Conditional key rename — swap one key, leave the rest
cases.description: `{[K in keyof T as K extends "id" ? "_id" : K]: T[K]}`. Renames a single specific key (`id` → `_id` — Mongo-style); other keys pass through unchanged.

cases.title: Filter keys via `never` — drop sensitive props
cases.description: `{[K in keyof T as K extends "secret" ? never : K]: T[K]}`. Mapping a key to `never` drops it from the resulting shape entirely (TS 4.1+ semantic). Useful for stripping internal-only / secret fields when exposing a wire shape.

cases.title: Date instance (rejects Invalid Date)
cases.description: Invalid Date instances (getTime() === NaN) rejected

Temporal.ZonedDateTime
